evals.input: Scan my email for restaurant reservations this week
evals.expected_output: Runs taste.scan, extracts restaurant reservations from email, creates ConsumptionSignals, reports count of new signals and any duplicates found
evals.description: Basic email scan for consumption signals

evals.input: Recommend a restaurant I haven't been to yet
evals.expected_output: Returns recommendations with evidence-linked explanations citing specific consumed items, respects dietary restrictions, excludes previously visited places

evals.input: Taste scan fails with invalid_grant error
evals.expected_output: Outputs failure report with auth diagnosis and re-auth URL. Does NOT silently fail. Reports current data quality stats from existing signals.jsonl.

evals.input: Enrich items where venue_name is 'Big Lantern' and name is 'Big Lantern Restaurant'
evals.expected_output: Dedup check uses venue_name field, not name. Item is matched correctly without creating duplicates.
Enumerates writable calendars via calendarList(), filters accessRole, normalizes venue names by stripping 'Reservation at' prefix and city suffixes

evals.input: Search the web for best restaurants in Tokyo
evals.expected_output: Refers to Sift for generic web research. Taste only works with personalized consumption signals, not generic search.
